claim: Cancer is caused by a deficiency of vitamin “B17,” a condition that can be remedied with nutritional supplements.

result.claim: Cancer is caused by a deficiency of vitamin “B17,” a condition that can be remedied with nutritional supplements.
result.reasoning: The claim that cancer is caused by a deficiency of vitamin "B17" and can be remedied with nutritional supplements is directly refuted by evidence from a clinical trial on Amygdalin (Laetrile), which is often referred to as vitamin B17. Paper ID 14, "A Clinical Trial of Amygdalin (Laetrile) in the Treatment of Human Cancer," explicitly states that "Amygdalin (Laetrile) is not effective as a cancer treatment" (prop_97) and that "No substantive benefit was observed in terms of cure, improvement, or stabilization of cancer" (prop_94). Furthermore, the paper highlights the toxicity of Amygdalin, noting that it is "a toxic drug" (prop_96) and that "The hazards of amygdalin therapy were evidenced in several patients by symptoms of cyanide toxicity or by blood cyanide levels approaching the lethal range" (chunk_18). No evidence was found to support the claim that cancer is caused by a vitamin B17 deficiency.
